_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to explain different historical interpretations — the different ways people understand and tell stories about the past.

_source.w: We are learning to explain different historical interpretations — the different ways people understand and tell stories about the past.
y: Recognising that the past can be interpreted differently helps us think more critically about what we read.
Understanding why interpretations differ builds our ability to weigh evidence and form balanced views.
This skill is essential for engaging with contested histories like colonisation and migration.
eg: Explaining why the Eureka Stockade is interpreted differently by different historians.
Comparing how a colonial development is described in an old textbook versus a modern one.
Discussing how new evidence or new perspectives can change an interpretation.
Explaining why the same event might be celebrated by one group and mourned by another.
Investigating how Aboriginal and non-Aboriginal historians interpret the same period differently.
Presenting two different interpretations of a historical development and explaining why they differ.
